reasoning: Given the current state, we have two resistors R1 and R2 in series. The total resistance of two resistors in series can be calculated by adding their individual resistances. However, to confirm this relationship, we should alter one of the resistances and check if the total resistance changes accordingly.

Increasing R1 to 30 ohms and observing the change in total resistance will help confirm if the resistors are in series. If total resistance increases by the same amount as R1, it would support the hypothesis of resistors in series.
